created a minimal working example for a problem in the regular expression engine. I was able to reproduce the issue on the latest stable release 1.8.3, on 1.9.0-alpha1, and on master built today. It seems to be a problem with the engine failing to backtrack.

```julia
julia> match(r"a+[bc]+c", "ababc")

julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.0-DEV.156
Commit 9be3c85e49 (2022-12-19 21:17 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 72 × Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 6150 CPU @ 2.70GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, skylake-avx512)
  Threads: 1 on 72 virtual cores
```

```julia
julia> match(r"a+[bc]+c", "ababc")

julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.8.3
Commit 0434deb161 (2022-11-14 20:14 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 72 × Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 6150 CPU @ 2.70GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-13.0.1 (ORCJIT, skylake-avx512)
  Threads: 1 on 72 virtual cores
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47936/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47936/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1359134792","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47936#issuecomment-1359134792","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47936","id":1359134792,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5RAsBI","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-12-20T10:21:30Z","updated_at":"2022-12-20T10:21:30Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"FWIW, the regex seems to work in pcre2 10.40:

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478","id":1438014004,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5Vtlo0","number":47478,"title":"Information about the behavior of lowercase","user":{"login":"rossati","id":521320,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUyMTMyMA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/521320?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati","html_url":"https://github.com/rossati","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rossati/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-11-07T09:09:11Z","updated_at":"2022-11-07T09:24:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"hello
debugging a my programs I ran into lowercasing a string with mixed latin and greek character, below the case:
```
julia> Greek = "Α:Alfa|Β:Beta|Δ:Delta|Γ:Gamma"
"Α:Alfa|Β:Beta|Δ:Delta|Γ:Gamma"

julia> lowercase(Greek)
"α:alfa|β:beta|δ:delta|γ:gamma"

julia>
```
It is correct? why only A and B followed by a colon they have been modified?
Thanks
Giovanni Rossati


","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1305322912","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478#issuecomment-1305322912","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47478","id":1305322912,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5NzaWg","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-11-07T09:24:09Z","updated_at":"2022-11-07T09:24:09Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"All the capitalized characters were modified, right? `Α` => `α`, `A` => `a`, ...

Note that (the first) `Α` is capital *alpha*, while (the second) `A` is capital *a*:
```
julia> Greek = "Α:Alfa|Β:Beta|Δ:Delta|Γ:Gamma"
"Α:Alfa|Β:Beta|Δ:Delta|Γ:Gamma"

julia> for i in eachindex(Greek)
           @info i Greek[i]
       end
┌ Info: 1
└   Greek[i] = 'Α': Unicode U+0391 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)
┌ Info: 3
└   Greek[i] = ':': ASCII/Unicode U+003A (category Po: Punctuation, other)
┌ Info: 4
└   Greek[i] = 'A': ASCII/Unicode U+0041 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)
[...]
```

Similarly for `Β` (*beta*) and `B` (*b*). 

Was that the source of confusion, or am I missing something?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1305322912/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The letter that looks like capital *a* is in fact capital *alpha*, and the one that looks like capital *b* is in fact capital *beta*.
```
julia> Alpha = 'Α'
'Α': Unicode U+0391 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)

julia> Beta = 'Β'
'Β': Unicode U+0392 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)

julia> A = 'A'
'A': ASCII/Unicode U+0041 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)

julia> B = 'B'
'B': ASCII/Unicode U+0042 (category Lu: Letter, uppercase)

julia> Alpha == A
false

julia> Beta == B
false

julia> lowercase(Alpha)
'α': Unicode U+03B1 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> lowercase(A)
'a': ASCII/Unicode U+0061 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> lowercase(Beta)
'β': Unicode U+03B2 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)

julia> lowercase(B)
'b': ASCII/Unicode U+0062 (category Ll: Letter, lowercase)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1305860570/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704","id":1464222868,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5XRkSU","number":47704,"title":"Unexpected type promotion in pairs","user":{"login":"hannes-uppman","id":19895171,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5ODk1MTcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19895171?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman","html_url":"https://github.com/hannes-uppman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/hannes-uppman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-11-25T08:56:47Z","updated_at":"2022-11-25T10:05:57Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is not what I was expecting:

    julia> ["a" => 0.2, "b" => 2]
    2-element Vector{Pair{String, Float64}}:
     "a" => 0.2
     "b" => 2.0

Compare with:

    julia> [("a", 0.2), ("b", 2)]
    2-element Vector{Tuple{String, Real}}:
     ("a", 0.2)
     ("b", 2)

and:

    julia> Dict("a" => 0.2, "b" => 2)
    Dict{String, Real} with 2 entries:
      "b" => 2
      "a" => 0.2

Tested with Julia 1.6.7, 1.7.3, and 1.8.3 (same results).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1327275238","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704#issuecomment-1327275238","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47704","id":1327275238,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5PHJzm","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-11-25T10:05:57Z","updated_at":"2022-11-25T10:05:57Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"The `[ ... ]` syntax invokes `Base.vect` which documents (and tests) this behavior:
```
  vect(X...)

  Create a Vector with element type computed from the promote_typeof of
  the argument, containing the argument list.

  Examples
  ≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡

  julia> a = Base.vect(UInt8(1), 2.5, 1//2)
  3-element Vector{Float64}:
   1.0
   2.5
   0.5
```
and it appears to be present for some time: e0b02f276a57fd13ddb373cdf6273ee49d3e9be2

Even though I don't know what is the reason for this (I am sure someone will comment on that), it is unlikely to get changed. Your expected type can be forced though:
```julia
julia> Pair{String, Real}["a" => 0.2, "b" => 2]
2-element Vector{Pair{String, Real}}:
 "a" => 0.2
 "b" => 2
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1327275238/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Although it does not involve LibGit2, I think this (linux) example shows the problem:
```
cd `mktemp -d`
git init
git config credential.helper 'cache --timeout=86400'
git config credential.someurl.helper 'cache --timeout=86400'
echo '[credential "helperselector"]
        selected = manager-core' >> .git/config
```
The config file contains (`cat .git/config`):
```
[core]
        repositoryformatversion = 0
        filemode = true
        bare = false
        logallrefupdates = true
[credential]
        helper = cache --timeout=86400
[credential "someurl"]
        helper = cache --timeout=86400
[credential "helperselector"]
        selected = manager-core
```
Then the following query
```
git config --get-regexp credential.*\.helper
```
prints
```
credential.helper cache --timeout=86400
credential.someurl.helper cache --timeout=86400
credential.helperselector.selected manager-core
```

Adjusting the regex
```
git config --get-regexp credential.*\.helper$
```
helps:
```
credential.helper cache --timeout=86400
credential.someurl.helper cache --timeout=86400
```

credential:

```
[credential "helperselector"]
    selected = manager-core
```

which gets introduced by Git for Windows and fails our assumption about
what a credential helper is.

The commit also removes a test that mirrors what `credential_helpers`
does (otherwise, we would have to maintain the same regex at two places,
the definition of `credential_helpers` and the test case).

previous regex for credential helpers included even the following
credential:

```
[credential "helperselector"]
    selected = manager-core
```

which gets introduced by Git for Windows and fails our assumption about
what a credential helper is.

The commit also removes a test that mirrors what `credential_helpers`
does (otherwise, we would have to maintain the same regex at two places,
the definition of `credential_helpers` and the test case).

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085","id":1239943968,"node_id":"I_kwDOBOhtSc5J6Asg","number":3085,"title":"Stackoverflow if manifest contains references to removed drive","user":{"login":"SimonDanisch","id":1010467,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwMTA0Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1010467?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch","html_url":"https://github.com/SimonDanisch","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SimonDanisch/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-05-18T13:10:24Z","updated_at":"2022-05-19T08:24:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I moved a project to another drive, which I then removed, but the Manifest still contained one absolute path pointing to the old drive, which lead to: 
```julia
pkg> add MathTeXEngine
    Updating registry at `C:\Users\sdani\.julia\registries\General`
    Updating git-repo `https://github.com/JuliaRegistries/General.git`
   Resolving package versions...
ERROR: StackOverflowError:
```
The reason is, that `safe_realpath` recursion anchor is `ispath`, which returns false for paths from a removed drive.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1131393156","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085#issuecomment-1131393156","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3085","id":1131393156,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBOhtSc5Db7CE","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-05-19T08:24:37Z","updated_at":"2022-05-19T08:24:37Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I think the following has the same underlying issue:
```julia
julia> safe_realpath("")
ERROR: StackOverflowError:
Stacktrace:
  [1] exec(re::Ptr{Nothing}, subject::String, offset::Int64, options::UInt32, match_data::Ptr{Nothing})
    @ Base.PCRE ./pcre.jl:193
  [2] exec_r_data
    @ ./pcre.jl:210 [inlined]
  [3] match(re::Regex, str::String, idx::Int64, add_opts::UInt32)
    @ Base ./regex.jl:371
  [4] match
    @ ./regex.jl:369 [inlined]
  [5] match
    @ ./regex.jl:388 [inlined]
  [6] _splitdir_nodrive(a::String, b::String)
    @ Base.Filesystem ./path.jl:138
  [7] splitdir
    @ ./path.jl:132 [inlined]
  [8] safe_realpath(path::String)
    @ Main ~/julia/Pkg.jl/src/utils.jl:68
  [9] safe_realpath(path::String) (repeats 14503 times)
    @ Main ~/julia/Pkg.jl/src/utils.jl:69
 [10] top-level scope
    @ REPL[2]:1
```
When `ispath(path)` returns `false` for any "sub-path", which is the case with a disconnected drive, it eventually arrives at `splitdir(path) == ("", "C:")`. The empty string is then passed to `safe_realpath`, causing the stackoverflow. I will prepare a PR.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1131393156/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

#35495.

Add some custom TeX styles include:
- `cover.tex`: The main definition of the cover, used to replace the default `\maketitle` command.
- `custom.sty`: Load the macro package required for the cover, define the background image style, etc.
- `preamble.tex`: Replace the default preamble for inserting a custom cover.
- `logo.png`: logo of julia. (LaTeX does not yet support SVG)
- `cover-background.png`: Background image of the cover title, from julia's homepage.

----

- [PDF @ 8bb4a0d-remove slogan](https://drive.google.com/file/d/10ulYkxn7ueBThEB-dpx-Gr4WHt3Qg-nM/view?usp=sharing)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5158738/164200172-2bcb2a2e-c892-43d9-b1f5-dd24ada1be5c.png)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619","id":1169522520,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5FtX9Y","number":44619,"title":"Base64: decoding concatenation of two encoded streams.","user":{"login":"baxmittens","id":100423479,"node_id":"U_kgDOBfxXNw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100423479?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens","html_url":"https://github.com/baxmittens","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/baxmittens/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-03-15T11:04:56Z","updated_at":"2022-03-15T12:19:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello there,

I'm reading base64encoded and Zlib-compressed VTU-Files and I always base64-decoded the VTU-header and the data at once and this always worked until I found a case where it didn't.

I'm no expert in base64, I'm just using it and until now I thought that what I did was legit.

However, here is my example where it stopped working:

```julia
import Base64

enc_header = "EQAAAAAAAAAAgAAAAAAAAKBvAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAA0AAAAAAAAADQAAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAA0AAAAAAAAADQAAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAA0AAAAAAAAADQAAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAA0AAAAAAAAADQAAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAA0AAAAAAAAADQAAAAAAAAANAAAAAAAAAAzAAAAAAAAAA=="
enc_data = ""

var1 = vcat( Base64.base64decode(enc_header ), Base64.base64decode(enc_data )) #this works
var2 = Base64.base64decode(enc_header*enc_data ) #this usually seems to work too but in this case gives not the correct decoded answer

@assert length(var1) == length(var2)
```

So implementing the former variant is no problem, I'm just wondering that the latter seems to work in uncounted examples that I'm unsure if this also should work in general.

Greetz max
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1067921296","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619#issuecomment-1067921296","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44619","id":1067921296,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM4_py-Q","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-03-15T12:19:17Z","updated_at":"2022-03-15T12:19:17Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I think this is not generally possible due to the padding (`=`). Also, I believe that a valid base64-encoded string allows `=` only at the end. So if `enc_header` has trailing `=`, then `enc_header*enc_data` is not a valid base64-encoded string. 

Maybe others will correct me, but IMHO this is not a bug.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1067921296/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```
julia> round(Integer, NaN)
ERROR: InexactError: trunc(Int64, NaN)
Stacktrace:
 [1] trunc
   @ ./float.jl:805 [inlined]
 [2] trunc
   @ ./float.jl:364 [inlined]
 [3] round(#unused#::Type{Integer}, x::Float64)
   @ Base ./float.jl:369
 [4] top-level scope
   @ REPL[16]:1

julia> round(Integer, big(NaN))
0
```
I think it is due to [mpfr.jl#L280](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/v1.7.2/base/mpfr.jl#L280) calling `mpfr_get_z` which returns 0 in case of `NaN` or `Inf`:
>If op is NaN or an infinity, the erange flag is set, rop is set to 0, and 0 is returned.

Is `_unchecked_cast` the correct place to check for `NaN` or `Inf`?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1072507087/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This time I do! It's with a sort with a comparison function that's slightly simpler that what I'm using in practice.

```julia
julia> sortperm(rand(rand(1000), 100000), rev=true, lt = (a, b) -> (a < b) || (a == b))

signal (11): Segmentation fault
in expression starting at REPL[1]:1
getindex at ./array.jl:861 [inlined]
lt at ./ordering.jl:116 [inlined]
partition! at ./sort.jl:560
sort! at ./sort.jl:575
```

Full segfault message: http://ix.io/3T9p

-----

```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.7.1
Commit ac5cc99908 (2021-12-22 19:35 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)
  CPU: AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3970X 32-Core Processor
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-12.0.1 (ORCJIT, znver2)
Environment:
  JULIA_NUM_THREADS = 60

Before:
```
julia> insert!(LOAD_PATH, 2, mktempdir())
4-element Vector{String}:
 "@"
 "/tmp/jl_DRt9jd"
 "@v#.#"
 "@stdlib"

julia> using PyPlot
 │ Package PyPlot not found, but a package named PyPlot is available from a registry. 
 │ Install package?
 │   (@v1.9) pkg> add PyPlot 
 └ Select environment:
 > 1: `/tmp/jl_DRt9jd` (@)
   2: `~/.julia/environments/v1.9/Project.toml` (/tmp/jl_DRt9jd)
```

With the PR:
```
julia> insert!(LOAD_PATH, 2, mktempdir())
4-element Vector{String}:
 "@"
 "/tmp/jl_x6zoyq"
 "@v#.#"
 "@stdlib"

julia> using PyPlot
 │ Package PyPlot not found, but a package named PyPlot is available from a registry. 
 │ Install package?
 │   (@v1.9) pkg> add PyPlot 
 └ Select environment:
 > 1: `/tmp/jl_x6zoyq` (/tmp/jl_x6zoyq)
   2: `~/.julia/environments/v1.9/Project.toml` (@v#.#)
```

Fixes #3031.

Regarding the implementation -- does `Base.load_path_expand` ever return `nothing` if given a non-`nothing` input? If not, then the code get get simpler. (I just followed the previous implementation.)

Also another side question: has it been discussed what should happen if there are >9 editable_envs? In that case, `only("$(n)")` ```

 "@"

```

With the PR:
```
julia> insert!(LOAD_PATH, 2, mktempdir())

 │ Package PyPlot not found, but a package named PyPlot is available from a registry.

 │   (@v1.9) pkg> add PyPlot

 > 1: `/tmp/jl_x6zoyq` (/tmp/jl_x6zoyq)
   2: `~/.julia/environments/v1.9/Project.toml` (@v#.#)
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784","id":1184355253,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5Gl9O1","number":44784,"title":"Pass AbstractString types to reading functions","user":{"login":"adkabo","id":61999878,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTk5ODc4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61999878?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo","html_url":"https://github.com/adkabo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adkabo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-03-29T06:11:21Z","updated_at":"2022-03-29T08:22:43Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are many [reading functions](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/io-network/#Text-I/O) but only `read` has a method that takes a type. I would like to use alternative `AbstractString` types with these functions. 

- [x] `read`
- [ ] `readline`
- [ ] `readuntil`
- [ ] `readlines`
- [ ] `eachline`

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1081574069","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784#issuecomment-1081574069","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/44784","id":1081574069,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5Ad4K1","user":{"login":"barucden","id":13768925,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNzY4OTI1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13768925?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden","html_url":"https://github.com/barucden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/barucden/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-03-29T08:22:43Z","updated_at":"2022-03-29T08:22:43Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Can you clarify what the proposal is?

Do you mean to make those functions operate over strings, like this
```julia
readline("line1\nline2") => "line1"
```
? 
That would be breaking, since the methods currently accept filenames as the first argument. However, `IOBuffer` can be used for that:
```julia
readline(IOBuffer("line1\nline2")) => "line1"
```

Or do you wish to specify a subtype of `AbstractString` that should be returned by the functions (kind of like `read(io::IO, T)`)? Something like this:
```julia
readline(IOBuffer("line1\nline2"), MyStringType) => MyStringType("line1")
```

That seems unnecessary to have in base imho. Why not just use 
```julia
MyStringType(readline(IOBuffer("line1\nline2")))
```
?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1081574069/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
